cards.prompt: 100 tasks each Credit 10 under lock; the critical section prevents lost updates and WaitAll makes the total deterministic: 1000 + 100*10 = 2000
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; using System.Threading.Tasks; object gate = new object(); decimal balance = 1000; void Credit(decimal a){ lock(gate){ balance += a; } } var tasks = new Task[100]; for (int i=0;i<100;i++) tasks[i]=Task.Run(()=>{ Credit(10); }); Task.WaitAll(tasks); Console.WriteLine($"balance={balance}");

cards.prompt: A System.Threading.Lock reports IsHeldByCurrentThread False before the lock body, True inside it, False after
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; var l = new Lock(); bool outside = l.IsHeldByCurrentThread; bool inside; lock(l){ inside = l.IsHeldByCurrentThread; } bool afterr = l.IsHeldByCurrentThread; Console.WriteLine($"{outside} {inside} {afterr}");

cards.prompt: A Lock is reentrant: the same thread can enter it recursively (depth reaches 3) without self-deadlock; after fully exiting it is not held
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; var l = new Lock(); int depth = 0; lock(l){ depth++; lock(l){ depth++; lock(l){ depth++; } } } Console.WriteLine($"depth={depth} held={l.IsHeldByCurrentThread}");
